document.title: Textarea
document.description: A multi-line input component for capturing longer text content from users, such as comments or messages.
document.summary: A form input for capturing multi-line text with customizable styling and behavior (support JsonInput).

body.raw:
---

The `Textarea` component provides a flexible and customizable input field that can handle multi-line text entries. It can be styled to fit different layouts and is perfect for situations where users need to enter longer text, such as feedback forms, comments, or code editors.

---

## Usage

The `Textarea` component is designed for handling multi-line text input. It can be used in forms, feedback sections, or anywhere where users need to provide more extensive information. Below is a basic example demonstrating how to use the `Textarea` component effectively in your project.

<TextareaDemosUsage />

## Textarea Validate Json

If your `Textarea` is intended for handling JSON input or any structured data, validation is crucial. This section demonstrates how to use the `Textarea` component along with validation, ensuring that the data entered by the user is in a valid JSON format.

<TextareaDemosValidateJson />

---

**Tip**:

> Use the `resize` property to control whether the user can resize the textarea. It's great for keeping the layout consistent, especially on fixed-width containers.

**Note**:

> Ensure that proper validation is in place when using `Textarea` for sensitive input types, like JSON or code, to provide immediate feedback for users.

---

## API References

- [`mdn`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/textarea)

JsonInput is based on Textarea component, it includes json validation logic and option to format input value on blur.

Textarea will adjust its height when you give it a value that exceeds its initial height, this approach will help you more to review what you have typed previously.

### Styles API

| Styles API  | Type      | Default | Annotation                                    |
| ----------- | --------- | ------- | --------------------------------------------- |
| unstyled`?` | `boolean` | `false` | if `true`, all default styles will be removed |

### Props API

| Props API            | Type                             | Default          | Annotation                                                                                                                                |
| -------------------- | -------------------------------- | ---------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| autosize`?`          | `boolean`                        | `true`           | determines whether the textarea height should grow with its content                                                                       |
| serialize`?`         | `typeof JSON.stringify`          | `JSON.stringify` | function to serialize value into a string, used for value formatting                                                                      |
| deserialize`?`       | `typeof JSON.parse`              | `JSON.parse`     | function to deserialize string value, used for value formatting and input JSON validation, must throw error if string cannot be processed |
| validateJson`?`      | `boolean`                        | `false`          | options for JSON validation                                                                                                               |
| formatOnBlur`?`      | `boolean`                        | `false`          | options for formatting JSON when blurring                                                                                                 |
| validationError`?`   | `string`                         | `"Invalid JSON"` | message thrown if JSON invalid                                                                                                            |
| onValidationError`?` | `(error: Error \| null) => void` | `undefined`      | callback when validation fails                                                                                                            |

---

## Source Codes

Full working code example, including necessary markup and styles.
You can copy and paste this code directly to start using the component immediately.

<SourceCodes name="textarea" />

    }\r

    const value = event.target.value;\r

          event.target.value = formattedJson;\r
          onAutoSIze(event);\r
\r

            ...event,\r
            target: { ...event.target, value: formattedJson }\r
          });\r
        } catch (error: any) {\r
          console.log("JSON.parse", error);\r
        }\r
      }\r
      onValidationError?.(null);\r

  return (\r

function getEnv() {\r

}\r

document.title: Times
document.description: A utility component for displaying or selecting times, often used in time pickers or schedule-based inputs.
document.summary: A time utility for presenting or interacting with time values in the UI.

body.raw:
## Usage

Basic usage example to quickly see how the `Times` works.

<TimesDemosUsage />

## Properties

Interactive configurator to explore customization options for the `Times` component.

<TimesDemosConfigurator />

## API References

### Styles API

- `<TimesPosted />`

```ts
type T = "root" | "createdAt" | "updatedAt" | "interval";
```

| Styles API    | Type                                | Default     | Annotation                                |
| ------------- | ----------------------------------- | ----------- | ----------------------------------------- |
| unstyled`?`   | `Partial<Record<T, boolean>>`       | `false`     | if `true`, default styles will be removed |
| className`?`  | `string`                            | `undefined` | pass to root component `<div>`            |
| classNames`?` | `Partial<Record<T, string>>`        | `undefined` |                                           |
| style`?`      | `CSSProperties`                     | `undefined` | pass to root component `<div>`            |
| styles`?`     | `Partial<Record<T, CSSProperties>>` | `undefined` |                                           |

### Props API

- `<Times />`

| Props API  | Type                | Default     | Annotation |
| ---------- | ------------------- | ----------- | ---------- |
| time`?`    | `string \| Date;`   | `undefined` |            |
| diff`?`    | `Format["diff"]`    | `short`     |            |
| locales`?` | `Format["locales"]` | `en-US`     |            |

- `<TimesPosted />`

| Props API       | Type                                                         | Default     | Annotation |
| --------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ | ----------- | ---------- |
| `?`             | ``                                                           | ``          |            |
| diff`?`         | `Format["diff"]`                                             | `short`     |            |
| locales`?`      | `Format["locales"]`                                          | `en-US`     |            |
| withInterval`?` | `boolean`                                                    | `false`     |            |
| times`?`        | `{ createdAt?: string \| Date; updatedAt?: string \| Date }` | `undefined` |            |

---

## Source Codes

Full working code example, including necessary markup and styles.
You can copy and paste this code directly to start using the component immediately.

<SourceCodes name="times" />

document.title: Toaster
document.description: Provides an easy-to-use notification system for displaying toast messages, built with a highly customizable and accessible foundation.
document.summary: Displays transient notifications with flexible configuration options.

body.raw:
## Usage

Quick example to demonstrate how the `<Toaster />` works out of the box.

<ToasterDemosUsage />

---

## Properties

Use the interactive configurator below to explore available customization options — like position, duration, type, and animation.

<ToasterDemosConfigurator />

### Chaining Toasts

Display icon toast notifications and/or multiple toast notifications in sequence or in grouped logic.

#### Icon

Display icon in front of the message.

<ToasterDemosConfiguratorChain />

---

## Installation

Install the required peer dependency:

```bash
npm install sonner
```

> Oeri UI's `Toaster` component is powered by [`sonner`](https://sonner.emilkowal.ski/), a minimal toast notification library with great accessibility and performance.

---

## Configuration

Make sure to include the `<Toaster />` component inside your application layout — typically after your `ThemeProvider`.

```tsx title="app/layout.tsx" showLineNumbers caption="Add the Toaster in your root layout"
import { ThemeProvider } from "@/config/themes";
import { Toaster } from "@/ui/toaster";

export default function RootLayout({ children }: Readonly<{ children: React.ReactNode }>) {
  return (
    <html lang="en" suppressHydrationWarning>
      <body>
        <ThemeProvider>
          {children}
          <Toaster />
        </ThemeProvider>
      </body>
    </html>
  );
}
```

---

## API Reference

Toaster uses Sonner under the hood. For a full list of supported props and advanced usage:

- [`View Sonner API Docs`](https://sonner.emilkowal.ski/toaster#api-reference)

---

## Source Codes

Full working implementation you can copy and paste:

<SourceCodes name="toaster" />

\r
import { useTheme } from "next-themes";\r
import { Toaster as Sonner } from "sonner";\r
\r
export type ToasterProps = React.ComponentProps<typeof Sonner>;\r
\r
export function Toaster(_props: ToasterProps) {\r
  const { theme: _, position = "bottom-right", ...props } = _props;\r
  const { theme = "system" } = useTheme();\r
\r
  return (\r
    <Sonner\r
      {...props}\r
      position={position}\r
      theme={theme as ToasterProps["theme"]}\r
      className="toaster group"\r
      toastOptions={{\r
        classNames: {\r
          toast:\r
            "[--image:url(/assets/background/cover.webp)] group toast [&_*]:select-none [&_*]:pointer-events-none !z-[calc(121+var(--z-index,0))] group-[.toaster]:bg-background group-[.toaster]:text-foreground group-[.toaster]:border-border group-[.toaster]:shadow-lg bg-cover bg-no-repeat bg-center [background-image:linear-gradient(to_right,#cccccc91_0%,#f4f6ffe8_75%),var(--image)] dark:[background-image:linear-gradient(to_right,_#151619e0_0%,#151619_75%),var(--image)]",\r
          description: "group-[.toast]:text-muted-foreground group-[.toast]:whitespace-pre-wrap",\r
          actionButton: "group-[.toast]:bg-primary group-[.toast]:text-primary-foreground",\r
          cancelButton: "group-[.toast]:bg-muted group-[.toast]:text-muted-foreground",\r
          title: "whitespace-pre-wrap"\r
        }\r
      }}\r
    />\r
  );\r

document.title: Tooltip
document.description: A hover- or focus-triggered popup that displays additional information or hints related to a target element.
document.summary: A small popup that provides extra context or hints when hovering or focusing on an element.

body.raw: ## Usage Basic usage example to quickly see how the `Tooltip` works. ## Peer Dependencies Install required dependencies before using the `Tooltip`. These libraries handle the low-level mechanics to ensure smooth performance and features. ```bash npm install xuxi @radix-ui/react-tooltip ``` ## Properties Interactive configurator to explore customization options for the `Tooltip` component. ### Tooltip Inline ## Anatomy Tooltip requires a trigger as an `content` anchors, you can provide children strings, fragments, numbers, and several elements/components which will later be wrapped in a ` Lorem... ); } ``` 2. Using inline components will make your writing more concise. - without `asChild` property ```tsx showLineNumbers export function MyToggle() { return ( Button ); } ``` - with asChild property ```tsx showLineNumbers export function MyToggle() { return ( Lorem...}> ); } ``` 3. Using ref in the trigger component. ```tsx showLineNumbers export const MyToggle = React.forwardRef, React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef<"a">>((props, ref) => { return ( Lorem...}> ); }); MyToggle.displayName = "MyToggle"; ``` --- ## API References Tooltip uses `@radix-ui` under the hood. For a full list of supported props and advanced usage: - [`@radix-ui/react-tooltip`](https://www.radix-ui.com/primitives/docs/components/tooltip#api-reference) [![npmjs.com/~khoeriilham](https://badgen.net/npm/v/xuxi/?icon=npm)](https://www.npmjs.com/xuxi) [![@radix-ui/react-tooltip](https://badgen.net/npm/v/@radix-ui/react-tooltip/?icon=npm)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@radix-ui/react-tooltip) ### Styles API | Styles API | Type | Default | Annotation | | ----------- | --------- | ------- | --------------------------------------------- | | unstyled`?` | `boolean` | `false` | if `true`, all default styles will be removed | ### Tooltip API | Props API Tooltip | Type | Default | Annotation | | ----------------- | ---------------------------------------- | ----------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ | | asChild`?` | `false` | - | pass the `triggerRef` props to an element or component as a single child | | content`?` | `React.ReactNode` | `null` | pass children to `TooltipContent` as `React.ReactNode` | | contentProps`?` | `DetailedHTMLProps Content` | `"div"` | pass all attribute `TooltipContent` | | open`?` | `boolean` | `false` | change the original `open` and `onOpenChange` state with own approach | | onOpenChange`?` | `(value: boolean) => void ` | `openState` | change the original `open` and `onOpenChange` state with own approach | | sideOffset`?` | `number` | `0` | distance between `TooltipTrigger` and `TooltipContent` | | withArrow`?` | `boolean` | `false` | add arrow on `TooltipContent` | | touch`?` | `boolean` | `false` | allows `TooltipContent` to open when receiving a touch | | align`?` | `"center" \| "start" \| "end"` | `"center"` | only available on `"dropdown"` variant | | side`?` | `"top" \| "right" \| "bottom" \| "left"` | `"bottom"` | only available on `["dropdown", "drawer"]` variant | --- ### TooltipTrigger API | Props API TooltipTrigger | Type | Default | Annotation | | ------------------------ | ------- | ------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ | | asChild`?` | `false` | - | pass the `triggerRef` props to an element or component as a single child | --- ## Source Codes Full working code example, including necessary markup and styles. You can copy and paste this code directly to start using the component immediately.

import * as React from "react";\r
import { mergeRefs } from "@/hooks/use-merged-ref";\r

\r
interface UseTypingWords {\r
  placeholders: string[];\r
  duration?: {\r
    /** Pause between words @default 1000 */\r
    break?: number;\r
    /** Typing speed @default 200 */\r
    typing?: number;\r
    /** Erase speed @default 100 */\r
    deleting?: number;\r
    /** Pause before deleting @default 1000 */\r
    pauseBeforeDelete?: number;\r
  };\r
}\r
\r
export function useTypingWords({ duration, placeholders }: UseTypingWords) {\r
  const ref = React.useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);\r
\r
  React.useEffect(() => {\r
    if (!ref.current) return;\r
    let animationId: NodeJS.Timeout | null = null;\r
\r
    const getRandomDelayBetween = (min: number, max: number) => Math.floor(Math.random() * (max - min + 1) + min);\r
\r
    const setTextContent = (element: HTMLElement, text: string) => {\r
      element.textContent = text;\r
    };\r
\r
    // Function to type text sequentially\r
    const animateTextTyping = (currentText: string[], remainingText: string[], element: HTMLElement, onAnimationEnd: () => void) => {\r
      if (!remainingText.length) {\r
        onAnimationEnd();\r
        return;\r
      }\r
      currentText.push(remainingText.shift() ?? "");\r
      animationId = setTimeout(\r
        () => {\r
          setTextContent(element, currentText.join(""));\r
          animateTextTyping(currentText, remainingText, element, onAnimationEnd);\r
        },\r
        getRandomDelayBetween(duration?.typing || 200, duration?.typing || 200)\r
      );\r
    };\r
\r
    // Function to delete text sequentially (reverse typing)\r
    const animateTextDeleting = (currentText: string[], element: HTMLElement, onAnimationEnd: () => void) => {\r
      if (!currentText.length) {\r
        onAnimationEnd();\r
        return;\r
      }\r
      currentText.pop();\r
      animationId = setTimeout(\r
        () => {\r
          setTextContent(element, currentText.join(""));\r
          animateTextDeleting(currentText, element, onAnimationEnd);\r
        },\r
        getRandomDelayBetween(duration?.deleting || 100, duration?.deleting || 100)\r
      );\r
    };\r
\r
    const animatePlaceholder = (element: HTMLElement, placeholder: string, onAnimationEnd: () => void) => {\r
      const currentText: string[] = [];\r
      const remainingText = placeholder.split("");\r
\r
      // The process of typing then pausing before deleting\r
      animateTextTyping(currentText, remainingText, element, () => {\r
        animationId = setTimeout(\r
          () => {\r
            animateTextDeleting(currentText, element, onAnimationEnd);\r
          },\r
          duration?.pauseBeforeDelete || 1000 // Pause before starting to erase\r
        );\r
      });\r
    };\r
\r
    const startAnimation = (index: number) => {\r
      const element = ref.current;\r
      if (!element) return;\r
      const nextIndex = (index + 1) % placeholders.length;\r
      animatePlaceholder(element, placeholders[index], () => {\r
        animationId = setTimeout(() => startAnimation(nextIndex), duration?.break || 1000);\r
      });\r
    };\r
\r
    startAnimation(0);\r
\r
    return () => {\r
      if (animationId) {\r
        clearTimeout(animationId);\r
      }\r
    };\r
  }, [duration, placeholders]);\r
\r
  return ref;\r
}\r
\r
export interface TypingWordsProps extends UseTypingWords, Omit<React.DetailedHTMLProps<React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLElement>, HTMLElement>, "style" | "children"> {\r
  el?: React.ElementType;\r

  style?: React.CSSProperties & Record<string, any>;\r

\r
export const TypingWords = React.forwardRef<HTMLElement, TypingWordsProps>((_props, ref) => {\r
  const { el = "div", dir = "ltr", placeholders, duration, suppressHydrationWarning = true, withCursor, className, ...props } = _props;\r
  const typingRef = useTypingWords({ placeholders, duration });\r

      {...{\r
        ref: mergeRefs(typingRef, ref),\r
        dir,\r

        "data-animation": "TypingWords",\r
        className: cn(\r
          {\r
            "relative [display:ruby] after:content-[''] after:relative after:block after:-bottom-1 after:overflow-hidden after:bg-transparent after:border-solid after:h-[--cursor-h,20px] after:[border-right:var(--cursor-w,0.15rem)_solid_var(--cursor-color,#e34ba9)] after:animate-cursor-bar": withCursor\r
          },\r
          className\r
        ),\r
        ...props\r
      }}\r
    />\r
  );\r
});\r

document.title: Typography
document.description: A self-styled text component that renders semantic HTML elements (e.g., h1–h6, p, span, ul, li) with consistent typography, supporting both el and variant props for maximum flexibility.
document.summary: A semantic text component with built-in styling, customizable via element (el) or style variant.

body.raw:
## Usage

Basic usage example to quickly see how the `Typography` works.

<TypographyDemosUsage />

## Properties

Interactive configurator to explore customization options for the `Typography` component.

<TypographyDemosConfigurator />

## API References

### Styles API

| Styles API  | Type      | Default | Annotation                                    |
| ----------- | --------- | ------- | --------------------------------------------- |
| unstyled`?` | `boolean` | `false` | if `true`, all default styles will be removed |

### Props API

| Props API | Type                          | Default         | Annotation                                                                                                    |
| --------- | ----------------------------- | --------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| el`?`     | `React.ElementType`           | `div` \|`prose` | Determines the type of tag to be used.                                                                        |
| prose`?`  | `TypographyProps<T>["prose"]` | `undefined`     | Type of `prose` that matches the html tag will be created as a `<Typography>` element before `el` is defined. |

---

## Source Codes

Full working code example, including necessary markup and styles.
You can copy and paste this code directly to start using the component immediately.

<SourceCodes name="typography" />

`,(0,r.jsx)(n,{name:"app-context",code:`// required global.d.ts\r
"use client";\r
import * as React from "react";\r
import { Cookies, Theme } from "./types";\r
import { useDirection } from "@/hooks/use-direction";\r
import { useCookie } from "@/hooks/use-cookie";\r
\r
export type CookiesName = \`\${Cookies}\` | (string & {});\r
\r
type DirectedAppValue = {\r
  dir: Direction;\r
  theme: Theme;\r
  isOpenAside: boolean;\r
};\r
\r
interface AppContext extends DirectedAppValue {\r
  openAside: Booleanish;\r
  setOpenAside: (v: Booleanish) => void;\r
  /** default value = \`30\` (one month) */\r
  setCookie: (name: CookiesName, value: string, days?: number) => void;\r
  toggleDirection: () => void;\r
  setDirection: (dir: Direction) => void;\r
}\r
\r
const ctx = React.createContext<AppContext | undefined>(undefined);\r
\r
export function useApp() {\r
  const _ctx = React.useContext(ctx);\r
  if (!_ctx) throw new Error("main layout must be used within an <AppProvider>");\r
  return _ctx;\r
}\r
\r
interface AppProviderProps {\r
  children: React.ReactNode;\r
}\r
\r
function useCookieValues() {\r
  const [dir] = useCookie<Direction>("__dir", "ltr");\r
  const [theme] = useCookie<Theme>("__theme", "system");\r
  const [isOpenAside] = useCookie<boolean>("__is_open_aside", true);\r
  return { theme, dir, isOpenAside };\r
}\r
\r
export function AppProvider(props: AppProviderProps) {\r
  const { children, ...others } = props;\r
  const { theme, dir: initialDirection, isOpenAside } = useCookieValues();\r
  const { dir, ..._direction } = useDirection({ initialDirection });\r
  const [openAside, setOpenAside] = React.useState<Booleanish>(isOpenAside);\r
  const setCookie = React.useCallback((name: string, value: string, days = 30) => {\r
    const date = new Date();\r
    date.setTime(date.getTime() + days * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000);\r
    document.cookie = \`\${name}=\${encodeURIComponent(value)};expires=\${date.toUTCString()};path=/\`;\r
  }, []);\r
\r
  const contextValue = React.useMemo<AppContext>(\r
    () => ({\r
      theme,\r
      dir,\r
      openAside,\r
      setOpenAside,\r
      setCookie,\r
      isOpenAside,\r
      ..._direction,\r
      ...others\r
    }),\r
    [theme, dir, openAside, setOpenAside, isOpenAside, setCookie, _direction, others]\r
  );\r
\r
  return (\r
    <ctx.Provider value={contextValue}>\r
      <html lang="en" dir={dir} suppressHydrationWarning data-themeid-light="default" data-themeid-dark="default" data-theme="default">\r
        {children}\r
      </html>\r
    </ctx.Provider>\r
  );\r
}`,ext:".tsx",repo:"web/configuration/app-context.tsx"})]})}function d(l={}){let{wrapper:e}=l.components||{};return e?(0,r.jsx)(e,{...l,children:(0,r.jsx)(h,{...l})}):h(l)}function S(l,e){throw new Error("Expected "+(e?"component":"object")+" `"+l+"` to be defined: you likely forgot to import, pass, or provide it.")}return b(w);})();

document.title: global.d.ts
document.description: A TypeScript declaration file that extends global types or modules. Often used for custom types or extending third-party library types.
document.summary: A TypeScript file for augmenting global declarations or adding custom global types.

body.raw:
## Declare Type Utility types for object transformation, key manipulation, and type inference in TypeScript ### nullable ```ts type nullable = null | undefined; ``` Represents values that can be either `null` or `undefined`. --- ### falsy - [`learn`](https://www.learn-ts.org/en/Truthy_and_Falsy) ```ts type falsy = false | 0 | "" | null | undefined | (number & { __falsyNaN__: void }); ``` In TypeScript, as in JavaScript, a _"falsy"_ value is a value that is treated as false when encountered in a Boolean context, such as in an if statement or when using the logical NOT operator `(!)`. There are six falsy values in TypeScript: - `false`: The boolean value false. - `0`: The number zero. - `""` (empty string): An empty string. - `null`: Represents the absence of a value. - `undefined`: Indicates that a variable has not been assigned a value. - `NaN`: "Not a Number," often resulting from invalid mathematical operations (represented using a branded intersection type because TypeScript does not support `NaN` as a literal type). All other values in TypeScript are considered _"truthy,"_ meaning they evaluate to true in a Boolean context. This includes: - Any number other than 0, including negative numbers. - Non-empty strings. - Arrays (even empty arrays []). - Objects (even empty objects {}). - Functions. **Note:** This type is primarily for static type checking and should be used with caution if exact runtime validation of `NaN` is needed. --- ### RequiredKeys ```ts type RequiredKeys = T extends string ? T | `${T}.required` : never; ``` Expands a string type to include a `.required` suffix, indicating a key that is required. --- ### SplitKey ```ts type SplitKey = K extends `${infer Key}.required` ? { key: Key; required: true } : { key: K; required: false }; ``` Splits a string key into its base key and a `required` flag. If the key ends in `.required`, the flag is set to `true`. --- ### ExtractParsedKey ```ts type ExtractParsedKey = { [P in K]: SplitKey

extends infer R ? (R extends { key: infer K2; required: infer R2 } ? (K2 extends keyof T ? (R2 extends IsRequired ? K2 : never) : never) : never) : never; }[K]; ``` Extracts the keys from type `T` that match the parsed key format and are either required or optional, depending on `IsRequired`. --- ### ParsedKeyUnion ```ts type ParsedKeyUnion = { [P in K]: SplitKey

["key"] }[K]; ``` Maps a union of keys with optional `.required` suffixes to their base key strings. --- ### Join ```ts type Join = K extends string ? (P extends string ? `${K}.${P}` : never) : never; ``` Joins two strings into a dot-separated path, useful for generating deep key paths. --- ### Prev ```ts type Prev = [never, 0, 1, 2, ..., 21]; ``` Provides an array-based utility to calculate the previous number. Often used to limit recursion depth in utility types. --- ### IgnoringSuffix ```ts type IgnoringSuffix = Date | string[] | number[] | Array; ``` Defines types that should stop recursion when resolving deep paths. --- ### DeepValueType ```ts type DeepValueType = ... ``` Resolves the type of a deeply nested property from object `T` using a dot-separated string path `P`. --- ### Booleanish ```ts type Booleanish = boolean | "true" | "false"; ``` Represents values that are either `boolean` or their string equivalents. --- ### Direction ```ts type Direction = "ltr" | "rtl"; ``` Defines text directionality, either left-to-right or right-to-left. --- ### InferType ```ts type InferType = T extends (...args: any[]) => infer R ? R : never; ``` Infers the return type of a function type. --- ### DeepPaths ```ts type DeepPaths = ... ``` Recursively computes all possible deep paths in a nested object type `T`, returning them as dot-separated strings. Stops at arrays. --- ### Paths ```ts type Paths = ... ``` Computes dot-separated paths to all nested properties within object `T`, up to a maximum depth `D`. Stops when it reaches types defined in `IgnoringSuffix`. --- ### Merge ```ts type Merge = { [K in keyof T]: T[K] }; ``` Creates a new type by shallow-merging all properties of `T`. Useful for resolving type intersections. --- ### Nullable ```ts type Nullable | void = never, N extends nullable = null> = ... ``` Makes properties of `T` nullable. You can control which keys are required/optional using the `K` generic, and customize the nullable type via `N`. --- ### Except ```ts type Except = { [P in Exclude]: T[P] }; ``` Creates a type by omitting the specified keys `K` from type `T`. --- ### NonNullables ```ts type NonNullables = { [K in keyof Required]: NonNullable; }; ``` Ensures all properties in `T` are required and non-nullable. --- ### NonNullableConstructor ```ts type NonNullableConstructor = ... ``` Recursively makes all fields non-nullable. Special handling for arrays, functions, and dates. --- ### KeysConstructor ```ts type KeysConstructor = ... ``` Generates a union of string templates in the format `"key-subkey"` where `key` is from a tuple’s first element and `subkey` is each string key of the second element. --- ### export `{}` Required to ensure that the TypeScript file is treated as a module and applies the `declare global` block properly. --- ## Source Codes Full working code example, including necessary markup and styles. You can copy and paste this code directly to start using the component immediately.

`,(0,r.jsx)(l.figure,{"data-rehype-pretty-code-figure":"",children:(0,r.jsx)(l.pre,{tabIndex:"0","data-language":"tsx","data-theme":"moonlight",__rawString__:`cnx("hello", "world");\r
// Output: "hello world"\r
\r
cnx(() => "there", "dynamic");\r
// Output: "there dynamic"\r
\r
cnx(["extra", 0, false, "bar"]);\r
// Output: "extra bar"\r
\r
cnx(Boolean, Object, undefined, null, "", 0, NaN);\r
// Output: ""\r
\r
cnx("hello", true && "foo", false && "bar");\r
// Output: "hello foo"\r
\r
cnx(["foo"], ["", 0, false, "bar"], [["baz", [["hello"], "there"]]]);\r
// Output: "foo bar baz hello there"\r
\r
cnx("foo", [1 && "bar", { baz: false, bat: null }, ["hello", ["world"]]], "cya");\r
// Output: "foo bar hello world cya"\r
\r
cnx("foo", { primary: true, disabled: false }, ["extra", null, undefined], () => "dynamic");\r
// Output: "foo primary extra dynamic"\r
\r

document.title: Text Parser
document.description: A collection of utilities for parsing and formatting text, including case transformations, byte formatting, and more.
document.summary: Text transformation and formatting utilities.

body.raw:
### String Utilities

#### Overview

This library provides a comprehensive set of functions for string manipulation. It includes methods to transform, sanitize, truncate, and format strings, making it versatile for various text processing tasks in web and application development.

---

### Functions

#### transform

Transforms a string based on the specified transformation type.

**Parameters:**

1. `transform: "uppercase" | "capitalize-first" | "capitalize" | "lowercase"`
   Defines the type of transformation.
2. `words: string | undefined`
   The input string to be transformed.

**Returns:** A transformed string.

**Example:**

```ts
transform.uppercase("hello-world");
// "HELLO WORLD"

transform.capitalize("hello-world");
// "Hello World"
```

---

#### truncate

Truncates a string to a specified maximum length, preserving word boundaries and adding an ellipsis if truncated.

**Parameters:**

1. `word: string`
   The string to truncate.
2. `maxWord: number (default: 30)`
   The maximum length of the string.

**Returns:** A truncated string.

**Example:**

```ts
truncate("This is a long sentence that needs truncating.", 20);
// "This is a long..."
```

---

#### lowerCasePunctuation

Converts a string to lowercase, removes punctuation, and replaces spaces with hyphens.

**Parameters:**

1. `str: string`
   The input string.

**Returns:** A sanitized and formatted string.

**Example:**

```ts
lowerCasePunctuation("Hello, World!");
// "hello-world"
```

---

#### sanitizedWord

Sanitizes a string by converting to lowercase, removing special characters, and replacing spaces with hyphens.

**Parameters:**

1. `words: string | undefined`
   The input string to sanitize.

**Returns:** A sanitized string.

**Example:**

```ts
sanitizedWord("Hello, World!");
// "hello-world"
```

---

#### desanitizeWord

Converts a sanitized string back to a readable format, capitalizing words while preserving conjunctions.

**Parameters:**

1. `words: string | undefined`
   The input string to desanitize.

2. `separator: string | RegExp (default: " " )`
   The word separator in the input string.

**Returns:** A desanitized string.

**Example:**

```ts
desanitizeWord("hello-world");
// "Hello World"
```

---

#### compareWords

Compares two strings for equality, ignoring case and special characters.

**Parameters:**

1. `word1: string | undefined | null`
2. `word2: string | undefined | null`

**Returns:** boolean - Whether the strings are equal.

**Example:**

```ts
compareWords("Hello!", "hello");
// true
```

---

#### getFirstString

Extracts the first word from a string, split by spaces, hyphens, or tildes.

**Parameters:**

1. `name: string`
   The input string.

**Returns:** The first word in the string.

**Example:**

```ts
getFirstString("John-Doe");
// "John"
```

---

#### camelToKebab

Converts a camelCase string to kebab-case or snake_case.

**Parameters:**

1. `words: string`
   The input camelCase string.

2. `kebab: "underscores" | "hyphens" (default: "hyphens")`
   The desired output style.

**Returns:** A kebab-case or snake_case string.

**Example:**

```ts
camelToKebab("helloWorld");
// "hello-world"
camelToKebab("helloWorld", "underscores");
// "hello_world"
```

---

#### kebabToCamel

Converts a kebab-case string to camelCase.

**Parameters:**

1. `words: string`

The input kebab-case string.

**Returns:** A camelCase string.

**Example:**

```ts
kebabToCamel("hello-world");
// "helloWorld"
```

---

#### toPascal

Converts a kebab-case string to PascalCase.

**Parameters:**

1. `words: string`
   The input kebab-case string.

**Returns:** A PascalCase string.

**Example:**

```ts
toPascal("hello-world");
// "HelloWorld"
```

---

#### formatedProgress

Formats a numeric input as a string. Returns the input unchanged if it contains non-numeric characters.

**Parameters:**

1. `input: string | undefined`
   The input string.

**Returns:** A formatted string or null if input is undefined.

**Example:**

```ts
formatedProgress("42");
// "42"
```

---

#### splitWordsToArray

Splits a string into an array of objects, each containing a word as text.

**Parameters:**

1. `words: string`
   The input string.

**Returns:** An array of objects with the structure `{ text: string }`.

**Example:**

```ts
splitWordsToArray("hello world");
// [{ text: "hello" }, { text: "world" }]
```

---

#### Helper Functions

1. toUpper
   Converts the first letter of a string to uppercase.
2. toLower
   Converts the first letter of a string to lowercase.
3. removePunctuation
   Removes punctuation from a string.
4. combineConsecutiveHyphens
   Replaces multiple consecutive hyphens with a single hyphen.

---

#### HTML Character Entities

Provides a list of mappings for HTML character entities.

**Example:**

```ts
htmlCharacterEntities.find(entity => entity.char === "<");
// { char: "<", entity: "<" }
```

export function getInitials(name: string, limit: number = 3, conjunctions: string[] = DEFAULT_CONJUNCTIONS): string {\r

document.title: Units Converters
document.description: Converts between CSS units such as px, rem, and em to support responsive and scalable styling.
document.summary: Comprehensive set of functions for size manipulation.

body.raw:
### rem dan em

rem and em functions are converters for changing values ​​in various formats into values ​​with rem or em units.

#### Syntax

```ts
const rem: (value: unknown) => string;
const em: (value: unknown) => string;
```

#### Parameters

`value` (unknown): The value to convert. Can be a number, string, or supported expression (e.g. `calc`, `clamp`).

**Returns:**

- A string containing a value in rem or em units.

#### Details

1. If the input is a number, the function divides it by 16 to convert from pixels (px) to rem/em.
2. Strings that already have the same units are returned directly unchanged.
3. Supports complex strings such as calc, clamp, or a space- or comma-separated list of values.

#### Example of Usage

```ts
rem(16);
// Output: "1rem"

em("32px");
// Output: "2em"

rem("calc(100% - 10px)");
// Output: "calc(100% - 0.625rem)"
```

---

### px

The px function is used to convert a value to pixels or get a numeric value from a string in pixels, rems, or ems.

#### Syntax

```ts
function px(value: unknown): string | number;
```

#### Parameters

`value` (unknown): The value to convert. Can be a number or a string.

**Returns:**

- If value is a number, it is returned directly.
- If value is a string:
  If it contains px units, it is returned as a unitless number.
  If it contains rem or em units, it is converted to pixels assuming 1 rem/em = 16 pixels.
  If it contains an expression such as calc or var, it is returned as a string.

#### Example of Usage

```ts
px(16);
// Output: 16

px("1rem");
// Output: 16

px("2em");
// Output: 32

px("calc(100% - 10px)");
// Output: "calc(100% - 10px)"
```

### createConverter

This function is a utility to create a custom converter with specific units. For example, rem and em are created using this function.

#### Syntax

```ts
function createConverter(units: string, { shouldScale }?: { shouldScale?: boolean }): (value: unknown) => string;
```

#### Parameters

1. `units` (string): The units used by the converter (e.g. `rem`, `em`).
2. `options` (object) (optional):
   `shouldScale` (boolean): Specifies whether the conversion result should be scaled using the scaleRem function.
3. Returns: A converter function that accepts a value to convert to the specified units.

#### Example of Usage:

```ts
const pt = createConverter("pt");
pt(16);
// Output: "1pt"
```

### Advantages and Optimizations

1. `Flexible`: Supports various input formats such as numbers, strings, and complex expressions.
2. `High Compatibility`: Can be used for various unit conversion needs in CSS, including responsive units such as rem and em.
3. `Advanced Expressions`: Supports operations with calc, clamp, and other CSS functions.
4. `Scalability`: The createConverter function allows the creation of custom converters for other units in the future.

---

 * Converts a value to a \`rem\` unit with optional scaling.\r
 *\r
 * @param {unknown} value - The value to convert. Can be a number, string, or other types.\r

export const rem: (value: unknown) => string = createConverter("rem", { shouldScale: true });\r
\r

 *\r
 * @param {unknown} value - The value to convert. Can be a number, string, or other types.\r
 * @returns {string} - The converted value in \`em\` units.\r
 */\r
export const em: (value: unknown) => string = createConverter("em");\r
\r
/**\r
 * Converts a value to a pixel (\`px\`) unit or returns the original value if it cannot be converted.\r
 *\r
 * @param {unknown} value - The value to convert. Can be a number, string, or other types.\r
 * @returns {string | number} - The converted value in \`px\` units, or \`NaN\` if conversion fails.\r
 *\r
 * @example\r
 * px(16);\r
 * // Returns: 16\r
 *\r
 * px('1rem');\r

 * px('calc(100% - 50px)');\r
 * // Returns: 'calc(100% - 50px)'\r
 */\r
export function px(value: unknown): string | number {\r

\r

    return transformedValue;\r
  }\r
\r
  if (typeof transformedValue === "string") {\r
    if (transformedValue.includes("calc") || transformedValue.includes("var")) {\r
      return transformedValue;\r

    const matchedUnit = Object.keys(unitMap).find(unit => transformedValue.includes(unit));\r
\r
    if (matchedUnit) {\r

    }\r
\r

\r
/**\r
 * Creates a converter function for a specific unit (e.g., \`rem\`, \`em\`).\r
 *\r
 * @param units - The target unit for conversion (e.g., \`rem\`, \`em\`).\r
 * @param [options] - Options for the converter.\r

 */\r
export function createConverter(units: string, { shouldScale = false } = {}): (value: unknown) => string {\r

      return shouldScale ? scaleRem(val) : val;\r
    }\r

      }\r

    .match(/^calc\\((.*?)\\)$/)?.[1]\r
    .split("*")[0]\r
    .trim();\r

document.title: useClickOutside
document.description: A hook that detects when a user clicks outside a specified element, commonly used to close popups, dropdowns, or modals.

`,(0,r.jsx)(n,{name:"use-click-outside",code:`"use client";\r
import { useEffect } from "react";\r
\r
const DEFAULTEVENTS = ["mousedown", "touchstart"];\r
\r
export function useClickOutside(handler: () => void, refs: React.RefObject<HTMLElement | null>[], events: string[] = DEFAULTEVENTS) {\r
  useEffect(() => {\r
    const listener = (event: MouseEvent | TouchEvent) => {\r
      const { target } = event;\r
      const shouldIgnore = target instanceof HTMLElement && (target.hasAttribute("data-ignore-clickoutside") || (!document.body.contains(target) && target.tagName !== "HTML"));\r
      const shouldTrigger = refs.every(ref => ref.current && !ref.current.contains(target as Node));\r
\r
      if (!shouldIgnore && shouldTrigger) handler();\r
    };\r
    // @ts-ignore\r
    events.forEach(event => document.addEventListener(event, listener));\r
    return () => {\r
      // @ts-ignore\r
      events.forEach(event => document.removeEventListener(event, listener));\r
    };\r
  }, [handler, refs, events]);\r
}`,ext:".ts",repo:"web/hooks/use-click-outside.ts"})]})}function h(t={}){let{wrapper:e}=t.components||{};return e?(0,r.jsx)(e,{...t,children:(0,r.jsx)(d,{...t})}):d(t)}function u(t,e){throw new Error("Expected "+(e?"component":"object")+" `"+t+"` to be defined: you likely forgot to import, pass, or provide it.")}return E(C);})();

`,(0,r.jsx)(o,{name:"use-clipboard",code:`"use client";\r
import { useState } from "react";\r
\r
export function useClipboard({ timeout = 2000 } = {}) {\r
  const [error, setError] = useState<Error | null>(null);\r
  const [copied, setCopied] = useState(false);\r
  const [copyTimeout, setCopyTimeout] = useState<number | null>(null);\r
\r
  const handleCopyResult = (value: boolean) => {\r
    window.clearTimeout(copyTimeout!);\r
    setCopyTimeout(window.setTimeout(() => setCopied(false), timeout));\r
    setCopied(value);\r
  };\r
\r
  const copy = (valueToCopy: any) => {\r
    if ("clipboard" in navigator) {\r
      navigator.clipboard\r
        .writeText(valueToCopy)\r
        .then(() => handleCopyResult(true))\r
        .catch(err => setError(err));\r
    } else {\r
      setError(new Error("useClipboard: navigator.clipboard is not supported"));\r
    }\r
  };\r
\r
  const reset = () => {\r
    setCopied(false);\r
    setError(null);\r
    window.clearTimeout(copyTimeout!);\r
  };\r
\r
  return { copy, reset, error, copied };\r
}`,ext:".ts",repo:"web/hooks/use-clipboard.ts"})]})}function p(t={}){let{wrapper:e}=t.components||{};return e?(0,r.jsx)(e,{...t,children:(0,r.jsx)(d,{...t})}):d(t)}function u(t,e){throw new Error("Expected "+(e?"component":"object")+" `"+t+"` to be defined: you likely forgot to import, pass, or provide it.")}return y(C);})();

`,(0,r.jsx)(t,{name:"use-device-info",code:`"use client";\r
import { useEffect, useLayoutEffect, useState } from "react";\r
import { getOSAuto } from "./use-os";\r
\r
export interface DeviceInfo {\r
  os: string;\r
  userAgent: string;\r
  language: string;\r
  orientation: string;\r
  screenWidth: number;\r
  screenHeight: number;\r
  devicePixelRatio: number;\r
  isTouchDevice: boolean;\r
  publicIp: string | undefined;\r
  deviceMemory: number | undefined;\r
  hardwareConcurrency: number | undefined;\r
}\r
\r
export function useDeviceInfo(): DeviceInfo {\r
  const useIsomorphicEffect = typeof document !== "undefined" ? useLayoutEffect : useEffect;\r
  const [info, setInfo] = useState<DeviceInfo>({\r
    os: "undetermined",\r
    userAgent: "",\r
    language: "",\r
    orientation: "landscape-primary",\r
    screenWidth: 0,\r
    screenHeight: 0,\r
    devicePixelRatio: 1,\r
    isTouchDevice: false,\r
    publicIp: undefined,\r
    deviceMemory: undefined,\r
    hardwareConcurrency: undefined\r
  });\r
\r
  useIsomorphicEffect(() => {\r
    async function fetchPublicIp() {\r
      try {\r
        const response = await fetch("https://api.ipify.org?format=json");\r
        const data = await response.json();\r
        setInfo(prev => ({\r
          ...prev,\r
          publicIp: data.ip\r
        }));\r
      } catch (err) {\r
        console.error("Error fetching public IP:", err);\r
      }\r
    }\r
    async function fetchOs() {\r
      try {\r
        const model = await getOSAuto(navigator.userAgent);\r
        setInfo(prev => ({\r
          ...prev,\r
          os: model.device.name\r
        }));\r
      } catch (err) {\r
        console.error("Error fetching OS:", err);\r
      }\r
    }\r
\r
    const updates = (event?: Event) => {\r
      const target = event?.currentTarget as ScreenOrientation;\r
      setInfo(prev => ({\r
        ...prev,\r
        userAgent: navigator.userAgent,\r
        language: navigator.language,\r
        screenWidth: window.screen.width,\r
        screenHeight: window.screen.height,\r
        devicePixelRatio: window.devicePixelRatio,\r
        orientation: target?.type || "landscape-primary",\r
        isTouchDevice: "ontouchstart" in window || navigator.maxTouchPoints > 0,\r
        deviceMemory: (navigator as any).deviceMemory,\r
        hardwareConcurrency: navigator.hardwareConcurrency\r
      }));\r
    };\r
\r
    fetchPublicIp();\r
    fetchOs();\r
    updates();\r
\r
    window.addEventListener("resize", updates);\r
    window.screen.orientation?.addEventListener("change", updates);\r
    return () => {\r
      window.removeEventListener("resize", updates);\r
      window.screen.orientation?.removeEventListener("change", updates);\r
    };\r
  }, []);\r
\r
  return info;\r
}`,ext:".ts",repo:"web/hooks/use-device-info.ts"})]})}function f(n={}){let{wrapper:e}=n.components||{};return e?(0,r.jsx)(e,{...n,children:(0,r.jsx)(u,{...n})}):u(n)}function l(n,e){throw new Error("Expected "+(e?"component":"object")+" `"+n+"` to be defined: you likely forgot to import, pass, or provide it.")}return b(I);})();

body.raw:
## API References

### Props API

| Props API         | Type                                           | Default                          | Annotation                                                                                                          |
| ----------------- | ---------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| keepMounted`?`    | `boolean`                                      | ``                               | If set element will not be unmounted from the DOM when it is hidden, `display: none` styles will be applied instead |
| transition`?`     | `Transition`                                   | ``                               | Transition name or object                                                                                           |
| duration`?`       | `number`                                       | `250`                            | Transition duration in ms                                                                                           |
| exitDuration`?`   | `number`                                       | `250`                            | Exit transition duration in ms                                                                                      |
| timingFunction`?` | `string`                                       | `theme.transitionTimingFunction` | Transition timing function                                                                                          |
| mounted`?`        | `boolean`                                      | ``                               | Determines whether component should be mounted to the DOM                                                           |
| children`?`       | `(styles: CSSProperties) => React.JSX.Element` | ``                               | Render function with transition styles argument                                                                     |
| onExited`?`       | `() => void`                                   | ``                               | Called when exit transition ends                                                                                    |
| onExit`?`         | `() => void`                                   | ``                               | Called when exit transition starts                                                                                  |
| onEnter`?`        | `() => void`                                   | ``                               | Called when enter transition starts                                                                                 |
| onEntered`?`      | `() => void`                                   | ``                               | Called when enter transition ends                                                                                   |
| enterDelay`?`     | `number`                                       | ``                               | Delay in ms before enter transition starts                                                                          |
| exitDelay`?`      | `number`                                       | ``                               | Delay in ms before exit transition starts                                                                           |

import { DependencyList, EffectCallback, useEffect, useRef } from "react";\r
\r
export function useDidUpdate(fn: EffectCallback, dependencies?: DependencyList) {\r
  const mounted = useRef(false);\r
\r
  useEffect(\r
    () => () => {\r
      mounted.current = false;\r
    },\r
    []\r
  );\r
\r
  useEffect(() => {\r
    if (mounted.current) {\r
      return fn();\r
    }\r
\r
    mounted.current = true;\r
    return undefined;\r
  }, dependencies);\r

document.title: useDirection
document.description: Detects or controls the current text direction (LTR or RTL), useful for localization and layout.
document.summary: Handles reading and updating text direction (LTR/RTL).

body.raw:
## Usage

Basic usage example to quickly see how the `useDirection` works.

<UseDirectionDemosUsage />

## API References

- [`mdn`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Global_attributes/dir)

### Props API

| Props API           | Type             | Default | Annotation                                                                                                                  |
| ------------------- | ---------------- | ------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| initialDirection`?` | `"ltr" \| "rtl"` | `ltr`   | Direction set as a default value                                                                                            |
| detectDirection`?`  | `boolean`        | `true`  | Determines whether direction should be updated on mount based on `dir` attribute set on root element (usually html element) |

`,(0,t.jsx)(n,{name:"use-direction",code:`"use client";\r
import React from "react";\r
\r
export type Direction = "ltr" | "rtl";\r
export interface useDirectionProps {\r
  /** Direction set as a default value, \`ltr\` by default */\r
  initialDirection?: Direction;\r
  /** Determines whether direction should be updated on mount based on \`dir\` attribute set on root element (usually html element), \`true\` by default */\r
  detectDirection?: boolean;\r
}\r
export function useDirection(_dir: useDirectionProps) {\r
  const { initialDirection = "ltr", detectDirection = true } = _dir;\r
  const useIsomorphicEffect = typeof document !== "undefined" ? React.useLayoutEffect : React.useEffect;\r
\r
  const [dir, setDir] = React.useState<Direction>(initialDirection);\r
\r
  const setCookie = (name: string, value: string, days = 30) => {\r
    const date = new Date();\r
    date.setTime(date.getTime() + days * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000);\r
    document.cookie = \`\${name}=\${encodeURIComponent(value)};expires=\${date.toUTCString()};path=/\`;\r
  };\r
\r
  const setDirection = (direction: Direction) => {\r
    setDir(direction);\r
    document.documentElement.setAttribute("dir", direction);\r
    setCookie("__dir", direction);\r
  };\r
\r
  useIsomorphicEffect(() => {\r
    if (detectDirection) {\r
      const direction = document.documentElement.getAttribute("dir");\r
      if (direction === "rtl" || direction === "ltr") {\r
        setDirection(direction);\r
      }\r
    }\r
  }, []);\r
\r
  const toggleDirection = () => setDirection(dir === "ltr" ? "rtl" : "ltr");\r
\r
  return { toggleDirection, setDirection, dir };\r
}`,ext:".ts",repo:"web/hooks/use-direction.ts"})]})}function u(r={}){let{wrapper:e}=r.components||{};return e?(0,t.jsx)(e,{...r,children:(0,t.jsx)(a,{...r})}):a(r)}function h(r,e){throw new Error("Expected "+(e?"component":"object")+" `"+r+"` to be defined: you likely forgot to import, pass, or provide it.")}return b(y);})();

`,(0,o.jsx)(r,{name:"use-disclosure",code:`"use client";\r
import { useState, useCallback } from "react";\r
\r
export function useDisclosure(initialState = false, callbacks?: { onOpen?(): void; onClose?(): void }) {\r
  const { onOpen, onClose } = callbacks || {};\r
  const [opened, setOpened] = useState(initialState);\r
\r
  const open = useCallback(() => {\r
    setOpened(isOpened => {\r
      if (!isOpened) {\r
        onOpen?.();\r
        return true;\r
      }\r
      return isOpened;\r
    });\r
  }, [onOpen]);\r
\r
  const close = useCallback(() => {\r
    setOpened(isOpened => {\r
      if (isOpened) {\r
        onClose?.();\r
        return false;\r
      }\r
      return isOpened;\r
    });\r
  }, [onClose]);\r
\r
  const toggle = useCallback(() => {\r
    if (opened) close();\r
    else open();\r
  }, [close, open, opened]);\r
\r
  return [opened, { open, close, toggle }] as const;\r
}`,ext:".ts",repo:"web/hooks/use-disclosure.ts"})]})}function u(n={}){let{wrapper:e}=n.components||{};return e?(0,o.jsx)(e,{...n,children:(0,o.jsx)(d,{...n})}):d(n)}function O(n,e){throw new Error("Expected "+(e?"component":"object")+" `"+n+"` to be defined: you likely forgot to import, pass, or provide it.")}return w(v);})();

document.title: useDocumentTitle
document.description: Updates the browser's document title dynamically based on your component state or props.
document.summary: Dynamically sets the document title.

body.raw:
## Usage

Basic usage example to quickly see how the `useDocumentTitle` works.

<UseDocumentTitleDemosUsage />

## API References

- [`mdn`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/title)
- [`client apis`](https://react.dev/reference/react-dom/client#client-apis)

useDocumentTitle helps you dynamically redefine the document title displayed in the browser title bar or on a page tab.

The contents of a page title is very important for search engine optimization (SEO)! The page title is used by search engine algorithms to decide the order when listing pages in search results.

_**Note**:_ useDocumentTitle runs on the client side so the title you set will be applied when the page is mounted. So, you can create a more accurate initial title for the SEO of your web application, try to make the title as accurate and meaningful as possible!

Here are some tips for creating a good title:

- Go for a longer, descriptive title [`(avoid one- or two-word titles) `](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/title).
- Search engines will display about 50-60 characters of the title, so try not to have titles longer than that.
- Do not use just a list of words as the title (this may reduce the page's position in search results).

---

You can copy and paste this code directly to start using the function immediately.

document.title: useElementInfo
document.description: Provides information about a DOM element, such as size, position, or bounding box.
document.summary: Returns metadata and layout info of a DOM element.

body.raw:
## Usage

Basic usage example to quickly see how the `useElementInfo` works.

<UseElementInfoDemosUsage />

## API References

- [`getBoundingClientRect()`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/getBoundingClientRect)

Necessary to define a `ref` on the related element to pass `getBoundingClientRect()`.
The `rect` returned value is a DOMRect object which is the smallest rectangle which contains the entire element, including its padding and border-width.

windowSize returns the interior width and height of the window in pixels (that is, the width and height of the window's [layout viewport](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Layout_viewport)).
The read-only [innerWidth](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Window/innerWidth) and [innerHeight](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Window/innerHeight) property of the [Window](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Window) interface returns the interior height of the window in pixels, that includes the width of the vertical scroll bar, if one is present and the height of the horizontal scroll bar, if present.

---

You can copy and paste this code directly to start using the function immediately.

You can copy and paste this code directly to start using the function immediately.`}),`
`,(0,n.jsx)(t,{name:"use-element-info",code:`"use client";\r
import { useCallback, useLayoutEffect, useRef, useState } from "react";\r
\r
export type RectInfo = "x" | "y" | "width" | "height" | "top" | "right" | "bottom" | "left" | "scrollX" | "scrollY";\r
export type RectElement = Record<RectInfo, number>;\r
export type SizeElement = { h: number | "auto"; w: number | "auto" };\r
export type InitialInfo = { initial?: Partial<RectElement> };\r
\r
const round = (num: number) => Math.round(num * 100) / 100;\r
const safeValue = (value: number) => (isNaN(value) ? 0 : round(value));\r
\r
function debounce<T>(fn: Function, delay: number) {\r
  let timer: NodeJS.Timeout;\r
  return (...args: T[]) => {\r
    clearTimeout(timer);\r
    timer = setTimeout(() => fn(...args), delay);\r
  };\r
}\r
\r
export function useElementRect<T extends HTMLElement | null>(el: T | null, withSizeElement: boolean = false, debounceDelay: number = 100) {\r
  const [rect, setRect] = useState<RectElement>({ ...{} } as RectElement);\r
  const [size, setSize] = useState<SizeElement>({ h: 0, w: 0 });\r
\r
  const updateRectElement = useCallback(() => {\r
    if (!el) return;\r
\r
    const rect = el.getBoundingClientRect();\r
    if (!rect) return;\r
\r
    setRect({\r
      top: safeValue(rect.top),\r
      left: safeValue(rect.left),\r
      right: safeValue(rect.right),\r
      bottom: safeValue(rect.bottom),\r
      width: safeValue(rect.width),\r
      height: safeValue(rect.height),\r
      scrollY: safeValue(window.scrollY),\r
      scrollX: safeValue(window.scrollX),\r
      y: safeValue(rect.top + window.scrollY),\r
      x: safeValue(rect.left + window.scrollX)\r
    });\r
\r
    if (withSizeElement) {\r
      setSize({ h: safeValue(el.scrollHeight), w: safeValue(el.scrollWidth) });\r
    }\r
  }, [el, withSizeElement]);\r
\r
  const handleScroll = useCallback(debounce(updateRectElement, debounceDelay), [updateRectElement, debounceDelay]);\r
  const handleResize = useCallback(debounce(updateRectElement, debounceDelay), [updateRectElement, debounceDelay]);\r
\r
  useLayoutEffect(() => {\r
    if (!el) return;\r
\r
    const resizeObserver = new ResizeObserver(() => updateRectElement());\r
    const mutationObserver = new MutationObserver(() => updateRectElement());\r
\r
    resizeObserver.observe(el);\r
    mutationObserver.observe(el, { attributes: true, childList: true, subtree: true });\r
\r
    window.addEventListener("scroll", handleScroll, { passive: true });\r
    window.addEventListener("resize", handleResize, { passive: true });\r
\r
    updateRectElement();\r
\r
    return () => {\r
      resizeObserver.disconnect();\r
      mutationObserver.disconnect();\r
      window.removeEventListener("scroll", handleScroll);\r
      window.removeEventListener("resize", handleResize);\r
    };\r
  }, [el, handleScroll, handleResize, updateRectElement]);\r
\r
  return { rect, size };\r
}\r
\r
export function useElementInfo<T extends HTMLElement | null>(element?: T | null) {\r
  const [hovered, setHovered] = useState<DOMRect | null>(null);\r
  const [scrollPosition, setScrollPosition] = useState(0);\r
  const [scrollBody, setScrollBody] = useState(0);\r
  const [windowSize, setWindowSize] = useState({ width: 0, height: 0 });\r
  const [attributes, setAttributes] = useState<{ [key: string]: string }>({});\r
  const [elementName, setElementName] = useState<string>("");\r
\r
  const ref = useRef<T | null>(null);\r
  const resizeObserverRef = useRef<ResizeObserver | null>(null);\r
  const mutationObserverRef = useRef<MutationObserver | null>(null);\r
\r
  const el = element !== undefined ? element : ref.current;\r
\r
  const { rect, size } = useElementRect<T>(el, true);\r
\r
  useLayoutEffect(() => {\r
    const handleScroll = () => {\r
      const el = element !== undefined ? element : ref.current;\r
      setScrollPosition(el?.scrollTop || 0);\r
    };\r
\r
    const handleScrollBody = () => {\r
      setScrollBody(document.documentElement.scrollTop);\r
    };\r
\r
    const handleResize = () => {\r
      setWindowSize({ width: window.innerWidth, height: window.innerHeight });\r
    };\r
\r
    const observeElement = () => {\r
      const el = element !== undefined ? element : ref.current;\r
      if (el) {\r
        setElementName(el.tagName.toLowerCase());\r
        const attrs: { [key: string]: string } = {};\r
        for (const attr of el.attributes) {\r
          attrs[attr.name] = attr.value;\r
        }\r
        setAttributes(attrs);\r
      }\r
    };\r
\r
    const disconnectObservers = () => {\r
      if (resizeObserverRef.current) {\r
        resizeObserverRef.current.disconnect();\r
        resizeObserverRef.current = null;\r
      }\r
      if (mutationObserverRef.current) {\r
        mutationObserverRef.current.disconnect();\r
        mutationObserverRef.current = null;\r
      }\r
    };\r
\r
    handleResize();\r
    observeElement();\r
\r
    window.addEventListener("scroll", handleScrollBody);\r
    window.addEventListener("resize", handleResize);\r
    const el = element !== undefined ? element : ref.current;\r
    el?.addEventListener("scroll", handleScroll);\r
\r
    return () => {\r
      window.removeEventListener("scroll", handleScrollBody);\r
      window.removeEventListener("resize", handleResize);\r
      if (el) {\r
        el.removeEventListener("scroll", handleScroll);\r
        disconnectObservers();\r
      }\r
    };\r
  }, [element]);\r
\r
  const onMouseEnter = (event: React.MouseEvent<HTMLElement>) => {\r
    setHovered(event.currentTarget.getBoundingClientRect());\r
  };\r
\r
  const onMouseLeave = () => setHovered(null);\r
\r
  return {\r
    ref,\r
    rect,\r
    size,\r
    windowSize,\r
    scrollBody,\r
    scrollPosition,\r
    attributes,\r
    elementName,\r
    onMouseEnter,\r
    onMouseLeave,\r
    hovered\r
  };\r
}`,ext:".ts",repo:"web/hooks/use-element-info.ts"})]})}function h(r={}){let{wrapper:e}=r.components||{};return e?(0,n.jsx)(e,{...r,children:(0,n.jsx)(d,{...r})}):d(r)}function u(r,e){throw new Error("Expected "+(e?"component":"object")+" `"+r+"` to be defined: you likely forgot to import, pass, or provide it.")}return E(z);})();

document.title: useEyeDropper
document.description: Leverages the EyeDropper API to let users pick colors from their screen.
document.summary: Allows users to select colors from anywhere on their screen.

body.raw:
## Usage

Basic usage example to quickly see how the `useEyeDropper` works.

<UseEyeDropperDemosUsage />

## API References

- [`mdn`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/EyeDropper_API)

The EyeDropper interface represents an instance of an eyedropper tool that can be opened and used by the user to select colors from the screen.

This example shows how to open an eyedropper tool and wait for the user to either select a pixel from the screen, or press `Escape` to cancel the eyedropper mode.

---

## Source Codes

Full working code example, including necessary markup.
You can copy and paste this code directly to start using the function immediately.

<SourceCodes name="use-eye-dropper" />

import { useCallback, useState } from "react";\r
import { useIsomorphicEffect } from "./use-isomorphic-effect";\r
\r
interface EyeDropperOpenOptions {\r
  signal?: AbortSignal;\r
}\r
export interface EyeDropperOpenReturnType {\r
  sRGBHex: string;\r
}\r
\r
export function useEyeDropper() {\r
  const [color, setColor] = useState<string>("");\r
  const [error, setError] = useState<Error | null>(null);\r
  const [supported, setSupported] = useState<boolean>(false);\r
\r
  useIsomorphicEffect(() => {\r
    setSupported(typeof window !== "undefined" && "EyeDropper" in window);\r
  }, []);\r
\r
  const open = useCallback(\r
    (options: EyeDropperOpenOptions = {}): Promise<EyeDropperOpenReturnType | undefined> => {\r
      if (supported) {\r
        const eyeDropper = new (window as any).EyeDropper();\r
        return eyeDropper.open(options);\r
      }\r
      return Promise.resolve(undefined);\r
    },\r
    [supported]\r
  );\r
\r
  const pickColor = useCallback(async () => {\r
    try {\r
      const result = await open();\r
      if (result) {\r
        setColor(result.sRGBHex);\r
        setError(null);\r
      }\r
    } catch (e: any) {\r
      if (e.name === "AbortError" || e.message === "The user canceled the selection.") {\r
        setError(null);\r
      } else {\r
        setError(e);\r
      }\r
    }\r
  }, [open]);\r
\r
  return { supported, open, color, error, pickColor };\r
}`,ext:".ts",repo:"web/hooks/use-eye-dropper.ts"})]})}function u(o={}){let{wrapper:e}=o.components||{};return e?(0,r.jsx)(e,{...o,children:(0,r.jsx)(p,{...o})}):p(o)}function d(o,e){throw new Error("Expected "+(e?"component":"object")+" `"+o+"` to be defined: you likely forgot to import, pass, or provide it.")}return v(E);})();

`,(0,n.jsx)(t,{name:"use-fetch",code:`"use client";\r
import { useCallback, useEffect, useRef, useState } from "react";\r
\r
export interface UseFetchOptions extends RequestInit {\r
  autoInvoke?: boolean;\r
}\r
\r
export function useFetch<T>(url: string, { autoInvoke = true, ...options }: UseFetchOptions = {}) {\r
  const [data, setData] = useState<T | null>(null);\r
  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);\r
  const [error, setError] = useState<Error | null>(null);\r
  const controller = useRef<AbortController | null>(null);\r
\r
  const refetch = useCallback(async () => {\r
    if (!url) {\r
      return;\r
    }\r
\r
    if (controller.current) {\r
      controller.current.abort();\r
    }\r
\r
    controller.current = new AbortController();\r
\r
    setLoading(true);\r
    setError(null);\r
\r
    try {\r
      const response = await fetch(url, { signal: controller.current.signal, ...options });\r
      if (!response.ok) {\r
        throw new Error(\`Error: \${response.statusText}\`);\r
      }\r
      const result = await response.json();\r
      setData(result);\r
    } catch (err: any) {\r
      if (err.name !== "AbortError") {\r
        setError(err);\r
      }\r
    } finally {\r
      setLoading(false);\r
    }\r
  }, [url, options]);\r
\r
  useEffect(() => {\r
    if (autoInvoke) {\r
      refetch();\r
    }\r
  }, [refetch, autoInvoke]);\r
\r
  useEffect(() => {\r
    return () => {\r
      if (controller.current) {\r
        controller.current.abort();\r
      }\r
    };\r
  }, []);\r
\r
  const abort = useCallback(() => {\r
    if (controller.current) {\r
      controller.current.abort();\r
    }\r
  }, []);\r
\r
  return { data, loading, error, refetch, abort };\r
}`,ext:".ts",repo:"web/hooks/use-fetch.ts"})]})}function h(r={}){let{wrapper:e}=r.components||{};return e?(0,n.jsx)(e,{...r,children:(0,n.jsx)(u,{...r})}):u(r)}function d(r,e){throw new Error("Expected "+(e?"component":"object")+" `"+r+"` to be defined: you likely forgot to import, pass, or provide it.")}return v(C);})();

document.title: useFullscreen
document.description: Controls and tracks the fullscreen state of an element, with programmatic toggle support.
document.summary: Manages entering and exiting fullscreen mode.

body.raw:
## Usage

Basic usage example to quickly see how the `useFullscreen` works.

<UseFullscreenDemosUsage />

### With Ref

Given an element that you'd like to present in fullscreen mode (such as a <video>, <img>, for example), you can present it in fullscreen mode by calling its [`requestFullscreen()`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/requestFullscreen) method.

<UseFullscreenDemosWithRefUsage />

## API References

- [`mdn`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Fullscreen_API)

It's not guaranteed that the element will be put into full screen mode. If permission to enter full screen mode is granted, the returned [`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise) will resolve and the element will receive a [`fullscreenchange`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/fullscreenchange_event) event to let it know that it's now in full screen mode. If permission is denied, the promise is rejected and the element receives a [`fullscreenerror`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/fullscreenerror_event) event instead. If the element has been detached from the original document, then the document receives these events instead.

---

## Source Codes

Full working code example, including necessary markup.
You can copy and paste this code directly to start using the function immediately.

import { useState, useEffect } from "react";\r
\r
type LocationPart = "address" | "road" | "neighbourhood" | "number" | "suburb" | "village" | "town" | "city" | "county" | "state" | "country" | "zip";\r
export type CoordinateLocations = Record<"loading", boolean> & Record<"latitude" | "longitude", number | null> & Record<LocationPart, string | null>;\r
\r
export function useGeoLocation() {\r
  const [location, setLocation] = useState<CoordinateLocations>({\r
    loading: true,\r
    ...{}\r
  } as CoordinateLocations);\r
\r
  useEffect(() => {\r
    if ("geolocation" in navigator) {\r
      navigator.geolocation.getCurrentPosition(\r
        function (position) {\r
          const latitude = position.coords.latitude;\r
          const longitude = position.coords.longitude;\r
\r
          fetch(\`https://nominatim.openstreetmap.org/reverse?format=jsonv2&lat=\${latitude}&lon=\${longitude}\`) // use \`openstreetmap\`\r
            .then(response => response.json())\r
            .then(data => {\r
              const loading = latitude === null && longitude === null;\r
              const partOfAddress: CoordinateLocations = {\r
                latitude,\r
                longitude,\r
                address: data?.display_name,\r
                road: data?.address.road,\r
                neighbourhood: data?.address.neighbourhood,\r
                number: data?.address.house_number,\r
                suburb: data?.address.suburb,\r
                village: data?.address.village,\r
                town: data?.address.town,\r
                city: data?.address.city,\r
                county: data?.address.county,\r
                state: data?.address.state,\r
                country: data?.address.country,\r
                zip: data?.address.postcode,\r
                loading\r
              };\r
\r
              setLocation(partOfAddress);\r
            })\r
            .catch(error => {\r
              console.error("Error getting user address:", error);\r
            });\r
        },\r
        function (error) {\r
          console.error("Error getting user location:", error.message);\r
        }\r
      );\r
    } else {\r
      console.error("Geolocation is not supported in this browser.");\r
    }\r
  }, []);\r
\r
  return location;\r

`,(0,n.jsx)(r,{name:"use-hotkeys",code:`"use client";\r
import { useEffect } from "react";\r
\r
export type KeyboardModifiers = {\r
  alt: boolean;\r
  ctrl: boolean;\r
  meta: boolean;\r
  mod: boolean;\r
  shift: boolean;\r
};\r
\r
export type Hotkey = KeyboardModifiers & {\r
  key?: string;\r
};\r
\r
export interface HotkeyItemOptions {\r
  preventDefault?: boolean;\r
}\r
\r
export type HotkeyItem = [string, (event: React.KeyboardEvent<HTMLElement> | KeyboardEvent) => void, HotkeyItemOptions?];\r
\r
type CheckHotkeyMatch = (event: KeyboardEvent) => boolean;\r
\r
export function parseHotkey(hotkey: string): Hotkey {\r
  const keys = hotkey\r
    .toLowerCase()\r
    .split("+")\r
    .map(part => part.trim());\r
\r
  const modifiers: KeyboardModifiers = {\r
    alt: keys.includes("alt"),\r
    ctrl: keys.includes("ctrl"),\r
    meta: keys.includes("meta"),\r
    mod: keys.includes("mod"),\r
    shift: keys.includes("shift")\r
  };\r
\r
  const reservedKeys = ["alt", "ctrl", "meta", "shift", "mod"];\r
  const freeKey = keys.find(key => !reservedKeys.includes(key));\r
\r
  return {\r
    ...modifiers,\r
    key: freeKey\r
  };\r
}\r
\r
function isExactHotkey(hotkey: Hotkey, event: KeyboardEvent): boolean {\r
  const { alt, ctrl, meta, mod, shift, key } = hotkey;\r
  const { altKey, ctrlKey, metaKey, shiftKey, key: pressedKey } = event;\r
\r
  if (alt !== altKey) {\r
    return false;\r
  }\r
\r
  if (mod) {\r
    if (!ctrlKey && !metaKey) {\r
      return false;\r
    }\r
  } else {\r
    if (ctrl !== ctrlKey) {\r
      return false;\r
    }\r
    if (meta !== metaKey) {\r
      return false;\r
    }\r
  }\r
  if (shift !== shiftKey) {\r
    return false;\r
  }\r
\r
  if (key && (pressedKey.toLowerCase() === key.toLowerCase() || event.code.replace("Key", "").toLowerCase() === key.toLowerCase())) {\r
    return true;\r
  }\r
\r
  return false;\r
}\r
\r
export function getHotkeyMatcher(hotkey: string): CheckHotkeyMatch {\r
  return event => isExactHotkey(parseHotkey(hotkey), event);\r
}\r
\r
export function getHotkeyHandler(hotkeys: HotkeyItem[]) {\r
  return (event: React.KeyboardEvent<HTMLElement> | KeyboardEvent) => {\r
    const _event = "nativeEvent" in event ? event.nativeEvent : event;\r
    hotkeys.forEach(([hotkey, handler, options = { preventDefault: true }]) => {\r
      if (getHotkeyMatcher(hotkey)(_event)) {\r
        if (options.preventDefault) {\r
          event.preventDefault();\r
        }\r
\r
        handler(_event);\r
      }\r
    });\r
  };\r
}\r
\r
function shouldFireEvent(event: KeyboardEvent, tagsToIgnore: string[], triggerOnContentEditable = false) {\r
  if (event.target instanceof HTMLElement) {\r
    if (triggerOnContentEditable) {\r
      return !tagsToIgnore.includes(event.target.tagName);\r
    }\r
\r
    return !event.target.isContentEditable && !tagsToIgnore.includes(event.target.tagName);\r
  }\r
\r
  return true;\r
}\r
\r
export function useHotkeys(hotkeys: HotkeyItem[], tagsToIgnore: string[] = ["INPUT", "TEXTAREA", "SELECT"], triggerOnContentEditable = false) {\r
  useEffect(() => {\r
    const keydownListener = (event: KeyboardEvent) => {\r
      hotkeys.forEach(([hotkey, handler, options = { preventDefault: true }]) => {\r
        if (getHotkeyMatcher(hotkey)(event) && shouldFireEvent(event, tagsToIgnore, triggerOnContentEditable)) {\r
          if (options.preventDefault) {\r
            event.preventDefault();\r
          }\r
\r
          handler(event);\r
        }\r
      });\r
    };\r
\r
    document.documentElement.addEventListener("keydown", keydownListener);\r
    return () => document.documentElement.removeEventListener("keydown", keydownListener);\r
  }, [hotkeys]);\r
}`,ext:".ts",repo:"web/hooks/use-hotkeys.ts"})]})}function u(t={}){let{wrapper:e}=t.components||{};return e?(0,n.jsx)(e,{...t,children:(0,n.jsx)(d,{...t})}):d(t)}function y(t,e){throw new Error("Expected "+(e?"component":"object")+" `"+t+"` to be defined: you likely forgot to import, pass, or provide it.")}return x(w);})();
